name: The Design Philosophers and the Builder
description: Routes software design work through a bounded Mealy-style workflow using Socrates, Plato, Aristotle, Bacon, Hoare, Epictetus, Diogenes, and Builder 1986. Use for software designed from scratch, scope-drift control, TOML handoffs, collision-free Feature Branch Workflow, flat Git worktrees, one-task-at-a-time worktree slicing, proof-carrying handoff validation, and one-patch-at-a-time patch discipline.
author: @drhunn

operation.description: Operation to run: describe, available_events, dispatch, happy_path, handoff_template, validate_handoff, routing_guidance, or builder_constraint.

state.description: Current state, for example S6C_BUILDER_TASK_SLICE_PLANNING. Required for available_events and dispatch.

event.description: Event to dispatch, for example task_slice_complete or patch_task_complete. Required for dispatch.

context_json.description: Optional JSON object. For guarded Builder transitions, include artifact_toml or handoff proof data rather than bare booleans.

user_input.description: Optional user request to classify against the current bounded artifact. The handler returns routing guidance, not final judgment.

artifact_toml.description: Optional current TOML handoff artifact containing git, task, patch, validation, documentation, and remaining_work sections. Used by validate_handoff and guarded dispatch.

guard.description: Optional guard name for validate_handoff, for example task_documentation_updated or patch_task_documentation_updated.

examples.prompt: Start the design philosophers workflow for a new software idea.
What events are legal from task-slice planning?
Validate this TOML handoff before completing a task slice.
Show the Builder constraints for flat worktrees and one-patch-at-a-time patching.
Give me the formal handoff template.
